Catalog Description

Computer Organization. (3-2). Credit 4. Computer systems from programmer's

perspective including simple logic design, data representation and processor

architecture, programming of processors, memory, control flow, input/output, and

performance measurements; hands-on lab assignments. Prerequisite: Grade of C

or better in CSCE 221, or concurrent enrollment.

Additional Course Details

Topics covered in the course are:

Computer System

Binary Representation

Boolean Logic Design

Arithmetic Logic Unit (ALU)

Sequential Design

Machine Language

CPU Architecture (Processor)

Memory

Virtual Memory

Assembler

Advanced Topics (based on time availability)

Course Prerequisites

Prerequisite/Corequisite(s):  Grade of C or better in CSCE 221, or concurrent

enrollment.

Course Learning Outcomes

Upon completion of the course, students will be able to:

1. Perform Basic Logic Design

2. Understand the design of a simple computer system

3. Understand elements that make up a CPU (Central Processing

Unit)

4. Design software with an understanding of how it will run on

computers

5. Participate in discussions that involve impact of hardware and

software elements on each other and the performance of the

program

6. Take intermediate and advanced classes in computer architecture,

systems, operating systems, cloud, parallel computing

Special Course Designation

None

Grading Policy

grade =

if (total score >= 90) then A

else if (total score >= 80) then B

else  if (total score >= 70) then C

else  if (total score >= 60) then D

else F

Exam and Assignment Weighting:

Midterm 1: 15%

Midterm 2: 15%

Midterm 3: 10%

Final Exam: 30%

Lab exercises: 25%

Lab attendance: 5%

Total: 100%

(extra credits) Thoughtful and constructive participation in class discussions

at instructor discretion: max of 3%

Late Work Policy

Work submitted by a student as makeup work for an excused absence is not

considered late work and is exempt from the late work policy ( Student Rule 7 ).

Course Specific Late Work Policy

No late submissions for assignments (i.e. lab exercises) will be allowed

without an excused medical or family event reason. To receive approval for a

medical or serious family event, you must send email to the TA, with

necessary documentation, such as a Dr's note or event note, no later than 3

days from assignment due date. You must receive approval form the TA. In

case you do not receive a response from the TA within 2 business days, send

me a reminder and cc the Instructor. An approval must be in place for full

credit relating to the missed assignment. For circumstances other than the

above, contact the instructor for approval.

For late submissions without excused extension the following reduction will

be applied:

80% of score received on assignment for late submission no greater

than 3 days from deadline

60% of score received on assignment for late submission more than 3

days but no greater than 1 week from deadline

40% of score received on assignment for late submission more than 1

week but no greater than 2 weeks from deadline

0% otherwise

AI Statement

You are not allowed to use any AI tool for any assignment, lab work,

exams or reports.

All detected plagiarism, cheating, and complicity will be reported to the

Aggie Honor System Office.

The sanction is a zero on the assignment and a letter grade reduction

for a single infraction.

The sanction can be a letter grade F or F* for a pattern of infractions.

Academic dishonesty will not be tolerated. For assignments, such as

status reports or poster creation, the group is expected to write their

material. Do not use AI tools! Not even for polishing your report or

correcting grammar. Use spellcheckers and grammar checkers in

document editors.

Students should not have another person/entity do the writing of any

portion of any assignment for them, which includes hiring a person or a

company to write assignments and using artificial intelligence tools like

ChatGPT, Gemini.

Every student should understand that complicity – helping or attempting

to help another student commit an act of academic dishonesty – also

constitutes academic dishonesty and carries the same punishment as

cheating and plagiarism.

Example if you have direct knowledge that another person on the class

is using AI tools for unapproved work, and you do not report it

immediately, you will be subject to the same sanction as the individual

committing plagiarism or unauthorized access of AI tools.

Course Specific Attendance Policy

Attendance is mandatory for all lectures and laboratories except for the

following situations:

For medical reasons, which requires documentation and an email to the

TA (for Lab) or to the Instructor (for Lectures)

For immediate family health reasons. Also requires documentation and

email to TA or Instructor.

2 unexcused absences are allowed for lectures and for labs each (ie a

total of 4).

Course Specific Makeup Work Policy

If you miss a mid semester exam, you will have to schedule a separate exam

with the TA within 1 week of the missed exam. Note that only documented

medical or family situations can be reasons for reschedule.

If you miss the final exam, contact the instructor immediately.

If you have 3 exams on May 1st (finals) contact the instructor for alternate

times no later than 10 days prior to the final exam.
